title: rubus villicaulis subsp. vulgaris
intro: Rubus villicaulis subsp. vulgaris, also known as the wild blackberry or thimbleberry, is a species of bramble that is native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant content, anti-inflammatory properties, and ability to support immune function. The health benefits of Rubus villicaulis subsp. vulgaris include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, and the fruit is also edible and can be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
